title: eriogonum strictum subsp. bellum
intro: Eriogonum strictum subsp. bellum, also known as Buckwheat Sedge or Balsam Sagebrush, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Eriogonum strictum subsp. bellum include its ability to soothe and protect the skin, reduce inflammation, and provide relief from coughs and colds. Eriogonum strictum subsp. bellum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies to promote overall well-being.
